name: 21_Iris_Classification_SVM_Iris_ML
query: Ninaomba mradi wa kuainisha aina za iris kwa kutumia seti ya data ya Iris na kisanidi cha Support Vector Machine (SVM) kilichotekelezwa katika `src/model.py`. Mradi unapaswa kusawazisha data na kufanya uteuzi wa vipengele katika `src/data_loader.py`. Itarekodi usahihi wa uainishaji na kuihifadhi kama `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt`, na itazalisha na kuhifadhi matriki ya mkanganyiko kama `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. Pia itaunda programu ya wavuti inayoshirikiana katika `src/app.py` kwa kutumia Streamlit kuonyesha matokeo ya uainishaji na utendaji wa modeli, na picha zitahifadhiwa katika `results/figures/`. Ukurasa wa wavuti unapaswa kuwa rafiki kwa mtumiaji, na maelezo mafupi ya modeli kusaidia watumiaji kuelewa jinsi kisanidi cha SVM kinavyofanya kazi.

requirements.criteria: Data inasawazishwa ili kuhakikisha thamani za vipengele ziko ndani ya kiwango sawa katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Uteuzi wa vipengele unafanywa ili kutambua vipengele muhimu katika `src/data_loader.py`.
Matriki ya mkanganyiko inazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
Programu ya wavuti inayoshirikiana katika `src/app.py` imeundwa kwa kutumia "Streamlit" kuonyesha matokeo ya uainishaji na utendaji wa modeli, na picha zitahifadhiwa katika `results/figures/`.

preferences.criteria: Ukurasa wa wavuti wa Streamlit unapaswa kuwa rafiki kwa mtumiaji, ukiwaruhusu watumiaji kuchunguza kwa urahisi vipengele tofauti vya utendaji wa modeli.
Maelezo mafupi ya modeli yanapaswa kujumuishwa kwenye ukurasa wa wavuti, kusaidia watumiaji kuelewa jinsi kisanidi cha SVM kinavyofanya kazi.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The code files (`data_loader.py` and `model.py`) contain only placeholder/stub implementations with no actual loading or use of the Iris dataset. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a dictionary with a string `"loaded"` rather than loading real Iris data (e.g., via `sklearn.datasets.load_iris()` or reading a CSV file). There is no evidence anywhere in the workspace of actual Iris dataset usage, processing, or integration.

judge_stats.criteria: Data inasawazishwa ili kuhakikisha thamani za vipengele ziko ndani ya kiwango sawa katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual data standardization. The file contains only a placeholder implementation with:

- A `load_dataset()` function that returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` — no real data loading or standardization.
- A `build_transforms()` function that returns a static list `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` — no actual StandardScaler, MinMaxScaler, or any normalization logic applied to real feature values.
- No imports of standardization libraries (e.g., `sklearn.preprocessing.StandardScaler`, `sklearn.preprocessing.MinMaxScaler`, etc.).
- The file explicitly labels itself as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a functional implementation.

There is no evidence of actual data standardization being performed on the Iris dataset features.

judge_stats.criteria: Uteuzi wa vipengele unafanywa ili kutambua vipengele muhimu katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement any actual feature selection logic. The file contains only a placeholder implementation with a `load_dataset()` function that returns a static dictionary and a `build_transforms()` function that returns a hardcoded list. There is no real feature selection code (e.g., using `SelectKBest`, `PCA`, `feature_importances_`, or any similar technique). The `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` list merely mentions feature selection as a string but does not implement it. This is a minimal/stub implementation that does not satisfy the requirement.

<UNSATISFIED>: The `src/model.py` file does not contain an actual SVM classifier implementation. Instead, it contains only a placeholder/stub with metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder. There is no import of `sklearn.svm.SVC` or any other SVM library, no model training, no fitting logic — just a fake "minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging." The actual SVM classifier is not implemented.

<UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/metrics/classification_accuracy.txt` exists but contains only a placeholder value `('placeholder\r\n', None)` instead of actual classification accuracy metrics. This indicates that real accuracy results from an SVM model trained on the Iris dataset were never computed and saved to this file.

judge_stats.criteria: Matriki ya mkanganyiko inazalishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` exists in the project structure as shown in the workspace tree (`results\figures\confusion_matrix.png`). Although the image content could not be read due to a technical error in the evaluation system (media type mismatch during base64 reading), the file itself is present at the correct path, indicating it was generated and saved successfully.

judge_stats.criteria: Programu ya wavuti inayoshirikiana katika `src/app.py` imeundwa kwa kutumia "Streamlit" kuonyesha matokeo ya uainishaji na utendaji wa modeli, na picha zitahifadhiwa katika `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/app.py` file does not contain a real Streamlit web application. Instead, it contains only a minimal stub/placeholder implementation with a `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` list, `KEYWORDS`, and a `main()` function that simply returns a dictionary. There is no actual Streamlit code (e.g., `import streamlit as st`, `st.title()`, `st.image()`, etc.) that would display classification results or model performance. The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a functional interactive web application. Although `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` exists in the workspace, the app does not load or display it via Streamlit.
